mission.title: Install and Configure Redis Operator (Spotahome / OT-CONTAINER-KIT)
mission.description: The Redis Operator by OT-CONTAINER-KIT simplifies the deployment and management of Redis instances on Kubernetes, supporting both standalone and clustered setups. It provides built-in monitoring and adheres to best practices for security and performance. This operator is ideal for teams looking to automate Redis management while ensuring high availability and scalability.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redis Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redis-operator https://ot-container-kit.github.io/redis-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the repository for the Redis Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Redis Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Redis Operator in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace redis-operator

helm install redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.19.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Redis Operator.
> Installs the Redis Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Redis Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all pods in the redis-operator namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Redis cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  mode: cluster
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Redis cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  mode: cluster
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the Redis cluster is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all Redis instances in the redis-operator namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Redis Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redis Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Redis instances managed by the operator, leading to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redis-operator --namespace redis-operator

kubectl delete namespace redis-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Redis Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Redis Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to back up any important data. This step will upgrade the Redis Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.19.0

kubectl uncordon <pod-name>

```

> Drains the Redis Operator pods.
> Upgrades the Redis Operator to the specified version.
> Uncordons the drained pods to allow scheduling.
commands: kubectl get pods -n redis-operator -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Redis instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured CRD.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account has the necessary permissions and that the CRD is correctly defined.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Redis configuration is incorrect or if there are resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for errors and adjust the configuration or resources as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Redis cluster is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to network policies or service misconfigurations.

**Fix:**
Verify network policies and ensure that the Redis service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Monitoring not working
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The redis-exporter may not be deployed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the deployment of the redis-exporter and ensure it is running and accessible.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Redis Sentinel mode, improved monitoring capabilities.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redis instances listed when running 'kubectl get redis -n redis-operator'. The status should indicate 'Running' for all instances.

kind: Redis
metadata:

  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  mode: cluster
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
